# Parrhēsia (frank speech)

Parrhēsia names frank speech: telling the truth plainly and without fear, even at the risk it brings before the powerful or the crowd. It is a public virtue of the Athenian citizen that the Cynics radicalize into moral provocation. Diogenes embodies it, challenging everyone with no regard for convention.

**Carries a stance toward** : [Sincerity](../axes/sincerity.md) : Truthful rigorism

**At stake in** : [Sincerity](../axes/sincerity.md)
